content.problemStatement:
Sequential or Linear search is a method to search for an element in an array. 

The algorithm for sequential is very simple and it works on unordered (unsorted) data as well.

To search for an element in an array, initialise an index to the start of the array and iterate it forward till you either reach end of the array or found the element your are looking for.

TaskComplete the implementation of the sequential search algorithm to solve this problem. 

Return the index of the element (1-based) if it exists in the array, else return -1.
constraints: Array elements are distinct and fit in 32‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and X, where N is the size of the array and X is the target element. The second line contains N distinct integers, the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the 1‑based index of X in the array if it exists; otherwise output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case the target 1 is at position 2 (1‑based). In the second test case the target 6 does not appear, so -1 is printed.
The target 3 is found at the third position.

solution.approach:
For each test case we scan the array from left to right. The first position i (0‑based) where arr[i] == X gives the answer i+1 (1‑based). If the scan finishes without a match we output -1. This is exactly the definition of sequential (linear) search and works for unsorted data.

Algorithm:
1. Read T.
2. Repeat T times:
   a. Read N and X.
   b. Read the N array elements.
   c. Initialise answer = -1.
   d. For i from 0 to N‑1:
        if arr[i] == X then answer = i+1 and break.
   e. Print answer.

The algorithm is optimal for this problem because any algorithm must inspect each element in the worst case when the array is unsorted.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the current array. Overall O(T * N_max) with N_max ≤ 1000.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input array (which is required).
